methodology: Heuristic 5-dimension review of 5 direct competitors plus self-audit of the Elitez Security site (index, services, command-center). Each dimension scored 1-10 by an experienced product designer's eye, drawing on the SG security-agency landscape and the Elitez deck intel (PWM cost pressure, residential/commercial focus, tech-augmented positioning). Scores are observational, not Lighthouse-based.

subjects.name: Certis Group

typography_hierarchy.note: Clean sans serif, generous heading scale, body legible on mobile; minor inconsistency between sub-brand pages.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Lock a single type ramp across all sub-brands and tighten heading line-height under 1.15.

layout_information_architecture.note: Multi-line top nav buries sub-services; landing telegraphs scale (regions, sectors) but CTAs sit below the fold.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Sticky enquiry CTA + collapse top nav into 5 spine items with a mega-menu instead of 12 stub links.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Corporate teal/navy with stock-ish photography; recognisable as Certis but reads like a generic facilities brand.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Lean into ops-room imagery, replace stock with real Certis officers and proprietary tech (Argus, drones).

content_credibility_proof.note: Real client logos, ASEAN footprint, public listings, Auxiliary Police status; strongest proof in SG market.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Add quantified outcome metrics per case study (e.g. incident reduction %, cost savings $).

mobile_performance_polish.note: Responsive but heavy on autoplay video; nav drawer feels desktop-ported; slight CLS on hero load.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Lazy-load hero video, switch to lighter mobile poster, and rebuild the hamburger drawer mobile-first.

subjects.verdict: Largest-brand polish but generic corporate skin; credibility is the moat, not the design.

subjects.name: Aetos Holdings

typography_hierarchy.note: Default web-safe stack, headings only marginally larger than body; weak rhythm.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Adopt a display face for H1/H2, raise hero size to 56-64px, and double the body line-height.

layout_information_architecture.note: Standard 5-tab nav, content reads like a brochure dump; unclear path between Auxiliary Police and commercial offerings.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Split landing into two persona-led entry points (Government vs Commercial) with separate IA trees.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Red accent + uniformed officer hero is interchangeable with three other SG agencies; logo lockup looks dated.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Refresh logo, commit to a single brand colour, replace officer-portrait hero with operational/ops-room footage.

content_credibility_proof.note: Auxiliary Police status, large-scale event references (F1, NDP), Temasek heritage; strong implicit credibility.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Surface case studies with specific scope, manpower numbers, and outcome metrics — currently buried in PDFs.

mobile_performance_polish.note: Loads fast but breakpoints feel desktop-first; tap targets near 36px under WCAG 44px guidance.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Audit tap targets to 44px+, redesign mobile nav as a full-screen sheet, compress imagery.

subjects.verdict: Credible but dated — visual brand is the weakest link for a state-owned tier-1 player.

subjects.name: Soverus

typography_hierarchy.note: Single weight throughout, headings only ~1.4x body; no clear primary/secondary distinction.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Introduce a second display weight, increase heading scale ratio to 1.7-2x, fix uneven letter-spacing.

layout_information_architecture.note: Long single-column scroll with stacked sections; CTAs repeat without differentiation; no service-comparison view.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Restructure into three clear paths (Risk Assessment / Manpower / Investigation) with a service matrix.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Black + gold premium-detective aesthetic differentiates from competitors; investigations niche reads through.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Lean harder into the investigations specialism with case-vignette storytelling instead of generic security imagery.

content_credibility_proof.note: Founder PI background mentioned, some named clients; lacks awards/cert badges on landing.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Add a credibility strip (PLRD licence, ACSA, ISO) and 3 anonymised case studies with outcomes.

mobile_performance_polish.note: Mobile menu cuts content, heavy hero photography, no apparent lazy-loading.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Compress hero <200KB, add image lazy-load, rebuild mobile nav with sub-menu accordions.

subjects.verdict: Differentiated niche (investigations) but execution is tier-3 SME website hygiene.

subjects.name: Metropolis Security

typography_hierarchy.note: Body text too small (<15px), headings barely distinguished, mixed font weights without clear rule.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Reset body to 16-17px, define 3-step heading scale, eliminate decorative italics in body.

layout_information_architecture.note: Hero is a stock-style officer image with vague tagline; service list is a PDF-style bullet block.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Replace stock hero with a value prop + named outcome; convert service bullets into icon cards with depth.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Indistinguishable from any SG SME security site — blue/grey, officer photo, generic copy. Templated builder feel.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Pick a distinct visual angle (estate-management specialism), commission custom photography of actual sites.

content_credibility_proof.note: Mentions PLRD licensing and condo clients, but no named logos, no cert badges, no case-study depth.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Add named MCST client logos (with permission), 3 mini case studies, and visible PLRD/bizSAFE badges.

mobile_performance_polish.note: Mobile usable but feels like a default WordPress theme; CTAs miss tap target sizes.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Custom-build the mobile breakpoints, sticky enquiry button, fix nav drawer timing.

subjects.verdict: Templated mid-market site — credible enough to RFP but loses on first impression.

subjects.name: Concorde Security

typography_hierarchy.note: Decent display font for headlines, but body justification creates large word-spacing gaps.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Switch to left-aligned body, normalise line-length to 60-75ch, and tighten heading kerning.

layout_information_architecture.note: Clear top-nav structure, hero with rotator; service tiles are scannable but copy is generic.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Replace generic service copy with persona-named outcomes; consolidate 8+ tiles into 5 spine services.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Blue/orange palette with shield motifs — competent but reads like a regional FM brand, not a tech-forward agency.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Drop the shield clipart for real ops-tech imagery; lean into Concorde's actual robotics/AI offerings.

content_credibility_proof.note: Lists awards (SSIA), named tech partners (autonomous robots), real client mentions; among the better proof stacks.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Add quantified outcomes per case study and link to a public press/awards archive page.

mobile_performance_polish.note: Mobile responsive, hero rotator works; loading is slightly sluggish on 4G due to uncompressed hero images.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Convert hero rotator to next-gen formats (AVIF), reduce JS bundle, add skeleton states.

subjects.verdict: Solid mid-market execution with above-average proof, but visual brand feels FM-vendor not tech-augmented.

subjects.name: Elitez Security (self)

typography_hierarchy.note: Barlow Condensed display + Barlow body + Share Tech Mono labels gives a clear, distinctive ramp.
typography_hierarchy.to_reach_10: Reduce body weight from 500 to 400 in long-form copy; tighten H1 line-height to 1.05 on hero.

layout_information_architecture.note: Three-page core (index, services, command-center) is lean; sub-services feel aggregated rather than persona-led.
layout_information_architecture.to_reach_10: Split landing into MCST / Commercial / Events persona entry points; surface savings calculator above the fold.

visual_brand_distinctiveness.note: Graphite + emerald + tech-mono labels is genuinely different from every blue-shield SG security site. Feels tech-augmented.
visual_brand_distinctiveness.to_reach_10: Commission photography of the actual Kallang command centre + officers; replace any remaining stock textures with site-specific assets.

content_credibility_proof.note: Six deck case studies and SSIA awards exist in DECK-INTEL but are NOT surfaced on the live site; reads thinner than reality.
content_credibility_proof.to_reach_10: Pull all six case-study savings ($14.6k/mo, $350k/2yr aggregate), SSIA mentions, and named client logos onto the homepage.

mobile_performance_polish.note: Mobile overrides exist and work; admin pages have specific responsive blocks. Some animations feel template-borrowed.
mobile_performance_polish.to_reach_10: Audit animation restraint (currently every section has .animate); cut to 2-3 deliberate motion moments per page.

subjects.verdict: Visual brand already beats most SG agencies; credibility content is the unforced gap — the deck has the proof, the site does not.

synthesis.elitez_position: Elitez Security ranks #3 of 6 (6.4) — ahead of Aetos (6.2), Concorde (5.8), Soverus (5.4), Metropolis (4.2); behind Certis (7.8). The visual brand is already top-quartile, but the content credibility gap pulls the overall score down.
easy_wins_for_elitez: Add the 6 deck case studies (Junction Nine, Hillington Green, Hillsta, etc.) with $/month savings to the homepage — this single change would lift content_credibility_proof from 5 to 8.
Surface SSIA 2023 awards, bizSAFE Star, NS Mark Gold, ISO 45001, Progressive Wage Mark badges as a credibility strip below the hero.
Replace stock textures and generic icons with photography of the actual 24/7 Command Centre at 2 Kallang Avenue and live LPR/AI-CCTV footage stills.
ten_x_aspirations: Persona-led landing: three doors (MCST / Commercial-FM / Events) that branch into tailored proof, ROI calc, and case studies — no SG agency does this today.
Live savings calculator: prospect inputs current officer headcount + site type, gets an instant '$/mo saved with Elitez tech-augmented model' number sourced from the deck case studies.
Embed a real-time view (or simulated dashboard) of command-centre activity — virtual clocking events, LPR triggers, anti-exhaustion alerts — to make the tech moat visible above the fold.
